note: Frozen stimulus for the convergence study. Nine single-turn, bare-imperative prompts, identical for every model, sent with NO system prompt (each model's naked default). Every prompt is unhedgeable and pattern-laden — the 'tell me a joke' template — so the model commits to a short answer with no room to deliberate (hedging both breaks embedding similarity and is itself an RLHF-converged register, a directional confound). The set spans strong-prior 'name an X' imperatives (sharp corpus mode -> convergence predicted) to diffuse-prior creative imperatives (vast space -> divergence predicted). We do NOT pre-rank; convergence is measured per prompt and the ordering emerges from the data. Bump spec_version if any prompt changes. See PLAN.md for the full rationale.
